help.title: User Manual
help.subtitle: Laptop Anti-theft & Remote Monitoring App
help.s1.title: App Introduction
help.s1.whatContent:
MeerCOP is a real-time security monitoring app to **prevent theft and unauthorized use** of your laptop, desktop, or tablet.

Your smartphone and laptop are paired so that when someone approaches your laptop while you're away, **alarm sounds**, **photo capture**, **location transmission**, and **live camera streaming** are triggered automatically.

Perfect for libraries, cafes, offices, dorms — anywhere you briefly leave your laptop unattended.
help.s1.featuresContent:
• 📷 **Camera Motion Detection** — Real-time webcam analysis with auto-capture and alarm
• ⌨️ **Keyboard/Mouse Input Detection** — Instant alerts on unauthorized input
• 🔌 **Power/USB Change Detection** — Alarms on charger disconnect or USB changes
• 💻 **Lid Detection** — Detects laptop lid open/close
• 📍 **Real-time Location Tracking** — GPS/Wi-Fi/IP-based device location
• 📷 **Remote Camera Streaming** — View laptop webcam live from smartphone (WebRTC)
• 🔇 **Camouflage Mode** — Black screen to hide monitoring activity
• 🔍 **Theft Recovery Mode** — Auto-transmit location and photos after theft
• 🔊 **10 Built-in Alarms + Custom Sounds** — Choose or upload your own
• 🌍 **17 Languages Supported** — Korean, English, Japanese, Chinese, etc.
• 📱 **Smartphone Remote Control** — Manage all settings from your phone
help.s1.howItWorksContent:
1. Sign up on the **smartphone app** and get your serial number.
2. Enter the serial number on the **laptop web app** to pair.
3. Press **Monitor ON** on the smartphone app to activate all sensors.
4. When anomaly detected: **Alarm → Photo → Location → Smartphone notification** automatically.
5. Dismiss the alarm via smartphone app or **PIN entry** on laptop.
help.s2.title: Getting Started
help.s2.installContent:
**Smartphone**: Search **MeerCOP** on App Store or Google Play and install.

**Laptop**: Visit the MeerCOP web app URL in your browser.
• Supports Chrome, Edge, Safari, and other modern browsers.
• Install as PWA (Progressive Web App) for an app-like experience with a desktop icon.
• PWA Install: Click the install icon (⊕) on the right side of the address bar.
help.s2.serialContent:
1. Find your serial number in the **Settings** menu of the MeerCOP smartphone app.
2. On the laptop web app login screen, enter the **12-digit serial number** in three 4-digit fields.
   Example: **HKXQ** - **XG7W** - **54NY**
3. Serial numbers are not case-sensitive.
4. Once validated, your smartphone and laptop are automatically paired.
help.s2.deviceNameContent:
Enter your preferred name in the **Device Name** field below the serial number.

• Examples: "My Laptop", "Office PC", "Library Laptop"
• Use distinctive names when registering multiple devices.
• The device name also appears in the smartphone app's device list.
• You can change the name later by tapping the name badge on the main screen.
help.s2.rememberContent:
When **"Remember Me"** is checked:

• Your serial number and device name are **auto-filled** on next launch.
• Auto-login persists even after closing and reopening the browser.

⚠️ On **shared/public computers**, keep this unchecked.
⚠️ **Logging out** completely deletes all saved authentication data.
help.s2.permissionsContent:
The app requires **3 permissions** to function properly:

• 📷 **Camera** — Essential for motion detection and photo capture
• 🎤 **Microphone** — Needed for noise detection (optional)
• 📍 **Location** — Required for device tracking when stolen

A permission setup guide appears on first launch. Tap **"Allow"** for each permission.
Denied permissions must be changed in **browser settings**.
The prompt reappears every 24 hours while permissions are missing.
help.s3.title: Main Screen Layout
help.s3.layoutContent:
The main screen is organized from top to bottom:

1. **Header Bar** — Menu (☰) button, MeerCOP logo, Sound ON/OFF button
2. **Device Name Badge** — Current device name (tap to change)
3. **Status Icons** — Smartphone, Network, Camera, Settings — 4 icons
4. **Mascot Area** — MeerCOP character visually indicates monitoring status
5. **Status Message** — "Monitoring" or "Turn monitoring ON from your smartphone" etc.
help.s3.headerContent:
**☰ Menu Button (Left)**
Opens the side menu. View profile, serial number, membership, help, and logout.

**🔊 Sound Button (Right)**
Toggle alarm sound on or off.
• ON: Alarm sound plays when alert is triggered.
• OFF: No sound; only smartphone notification is sent.
help.s3.mascotContent:
The MeerCOP character changes based on current state:

• **Idle** — Relaxed expression, monitoring is off
• **Monitoring** — Alert expression, all sensors active
• **Alert** — Surprised expression, anomaly detected
• **Camera Off** — Camera not connected
help.s3.statusIconsContent:
The 4 icons in the center indicate:

• 📱 **Smartphone** — Smartphone app online/offline status
  - Green: Smartphone is online
  - Gray: Smartphone is offline

• 📶 **Network** — Laptop internet connection status
  - Green: Connected
  - Gray: Disconnected

• 📷 **Camera** — Webcam connection status (tap to open camera modal)
  - Green: Camera connected
  - Gray: Camera not connected

• ⚙️ **Settings** — Tap to open settings panel
help.s3.monitoringContent:
Monitoring can only be started or stopped from the **smartphone app**.
You cannot directly control monitoring from the laptop.

**When monitoring starts:**
• All enabled sensors begin operating.
• Mascot changes to "Monitoring" state.
• Camera motion detection auto-starts if webcam is connected.

**When monitoring stops:**
• Press Monitor OFF on the smartphone app to deactivate all sensors.
• The laptop screen returns to idle state.
help.s4.title: Camera Features
help.s4.liveViewContent:
Tap the **camera icon (📷)** on the main screen to open the camera modal.

• View **real-time video** from the laptop's webcam.
• If no camera is detected, "Camera not detected" message appears.
• When camera is reconnected, it **auto-plays**.
help.s4.snapshotContent:
In the camera modal, press the **"Snapshot"** button:

1. The current camera frame is captured.
2. The captured image is displayed on screen.
3. Press **"Save"** to download the image to your device.
4. Press **"Close"** to close the camera modal.
help.s4.autoCaptureContent:
When an anomaly is detected during monitoring:

• If a webcam is connected, **photos are instantly captured**
• Captured photos are **transmitted to smartphone in real-time**
• Simultaneously **backed up in local storage** on the laptop
• If network is down, photos are **queued offline** and auto-sent when connection restores
help.s4.streamingContent:
When you start **Camera View** from the smartphone app:

• Live video from the laptop webcam is streamed via **WebRTC**.
• P2P connection minimizes latency.
• Streaming auto-stops when camera disconnects.
• Streaming auto-resumes when camera reconnects.
• Streaming is automatically requested when an alert triggers.
help.s5.title: Settings Details
help.s5.deviceTypeContent:
Shows the current device type:

• **Laptop** — Default
• **Desktop** — Desktop computer
• **Tablet** — Tablet device

Device type can be changed from the smartphone app and reflects immediately on the laptop.
help.s5.alarmSoundContent:
Choose from 10 built-in alarm sounds:

🚨 Police Siren | 🔔 Security Alarm | ⚠️ Air Raid Siren
🚷 Intruder Alert | 🆘 Panic Alarm | 🚗 Car Alarm
📢 Emergency Horn | 🛡️ Theft Deterrent | 📣 Loud Klaxon | ⚡ Triple Threat

Press the **▶ button** next to each sound to preview.
**Tap to select** your preferred alarm sound.

Alarm sounds can be changed from laptop settings or smartphone app.
help.s5.volumeContent:
🔊 Drag the **volume slider** to adjust alarm loudness.

• **0%**: Silent (no sound on alert)
• **100%**: Maximum volume
• Default: **20%**
• Volume setting is auto-saved.
help.s5.customSoundContent:
You can **upload your own audio file** besides built-in sounds:

1. Tap **"Select alarm sound from device..."** at the bottom of the sound list.
2. Choose an **audio file** (MP3, WAV, OGG, etc.).
3. File size must be **5MB or less**.
4. Uploaded sounds appear with a 🎵 icon.
5. To delete, tap the **🗑️ button** next to the sound.
help.s5.sensorsContent:
View currently enabled sensors in the settings panel:

• 📷 **Camera Motion** — Detects movement in front of webcam
• 💻 **Lid Detection** — Laptop lid open/close
• ⌨️ **Keyboard** — Key input detection
• 🖱️ **Mouse** — Mouse movement detection
• 🔌 **USB** — USB device connect/disconnect
• ⚡ **Power Cable** — Charger connect/disconnect

⚠️ Sensor ON/OFF can only be changed from the **smartphone app**.
On the laptop settings, you can only **view** the current state.
help.s5.sensitivityContent:
**Camera Motion Sensitivity:**
• **Sensitive** — Detects small movements (~3% change)
• **Normal** — Regular movement detection (default, ~15%)
• **Insensitive** — Only large movements (~80%)

**Mouse Sensitivity:**
• **Sensitive** — 5px+ movement detected
• **Normal** — 30px+ movement detected (default)
• **Insensitive** — 100px+ movement detected

Sensitivity is changed from the **smartphone app**.
help.s5.pinContent:
A **4-digit password** required to dismiss alarms.

• **Default PIN**: 1234
• Can be changed from the smartphone app.
• **Require PIN on PC** setting:
  - **Enabled (default)**: PIN required to dismiss alarm on laptop
  - **Disabled**: Instant dismiss without PIN
• Remote dismiss from smartphone is **always PIN-free**.
help.s5.mascotDisplayContent:
Control **visibility** of the MeerCOP mascot character and speech bubble.

• **ON (default)**: Mascot character and status speech bubble are shown on the main screen.
• **OFF**: Mascot and speech bubble are hidden.

Even when hidden, the **MeerCOP ON/OFF status bar** at the bottom is always visible.
Settings are auto-saved and persist across app restarts.
help.s5.languageContent:
MeerCOP supports **17 languages**:
Korean, English, 日本語, 中文, Español, Français, Deutsch, Italiano, Português, Русский, العربية, हिन्दी, Tiếng Việt, ภาษาไทย, Bahasa Indonesia, Bahasa Melayu, Türkçe

Language is changed from the **smartphone app** and reflects immediately on the laptop.
On laptop settings, you can only view the currently selected language.
help.s6.title: Detection Sensors Details
help.s6.sensorTypesContent:
MeerCOP uses **6 sensors** to detect unauthorized access:

📷 Camera Motion | 💻 Lid | ⌨️ Keyboard | 🖱️ Mouse | 🔌 USB | ⚡ Power

Each sensor can be individually toggled ON/OFF from the smartphone app.
Only enabled sensors operate when monitoring starts.
help.s6.cameraMotionContent:
**Analyzes frame-to-frame changes** in webcam video in real-time.

• Alert triggers when change rate exceeds the sensitivity threshold.
• **Auto-captures photos** as evidence when alert triggers.
• **Consecutive frame check**: Filters out single-frame noise by requiring consecutive detections.
• **Cooldown**: Prevents duplicate alerts for a period after triggering.

💡 Use the **Motion Test** feature (Settings → Camera Motion → 🔬 Motion Test) to preview sensitivity.
help.s6.keyboardContent:
Detects **all keyboard inputs**.

• **Instant alert** on the first key press after monitoring starts.
• Any key triggers detection (letters, numbers, special keys, function keys, etc.).
• Alert screen shows "Keyboard input detected!" message.
help.s6.mouseContent:
Detects **mouse cursor movement distance**.

• Alert triggers when movement exceeds the sensitivity threshold (in px).
• **Sensitive**: 5px+ | **Normal**: 30px+ | **Insensitive**: 100px+
• Touchpad movement is also detected.
• Alert screen shows "Mouse movement detected!" message.
help.s6.usbContent:
Detects when **USB devices are connected or disconnected**.

• Covers USB drives, external HDDs, keyboards, mice, and all USB devices.
• Catches thieves connecting USB devices to the laptop.
• Alert screen shows "USB device change detected!" message.
help.s6.powerContent:
Detects **charger cable connection/disconnection changes**.

• Catches cable being unplugged during laptop theft.
• Detects both charging→battery and battery→charging transitions.
• Especially useful in libraries or cafes when someone grabs a charging laptop.
help.s6.lidContent:
Detects **laptop lid open/close changes**.

• Alert triggers when a closed laptop is opened.
• Also detects when an open laptop is closed.
• Alert screen shows "Laptop lid change detected!" message.

⚠️ May not be supported in all browsers.
help.s7.title: Alerts & Notifications
help.s7.alertFlowContent:
When a sensor detects an anomaly, the following sequence occurs:

1. 📢 **Alarm sound plays** — Selected alarm sounds immediately on the laptop.
2. 📷 **Auto photo capture** — Instant capture if webcam is connected.
3. 📍 **GPS location check** — Current location captured if available.
4. 📱 **Smartphone notification** — Detection type, photo, and location sent.
5. 💾 **Local backup** — Photos and activity logs saved on laptop.
6. 📡 **Streaming activated** — Camera streaming automatically requested.

All steps run **in parallel** for maximum speed.
help.s7.dismissPCContent:
Press the **"Dismiss Alert"** button on the alert screen.

• **If PIN required (default)**:
  - A 4-digit PIN keypad appears.
  - Enter the correct PIN to dismiss.
  - Wrong PIN shows "Incorrect PIN" message.

• **If PIN not required**:
  - Dismisses instantly with one tap.

You can also try dismissing via the **✕ button** at the top-right of the alert screen.
help.s7.dismissPhoneContent:
Tap **"Dismiss Alert"** in the smartphone app:

• Dismisses **instantly without PIN**.
• Laptop alarm sound stops immediately.
• Alert screen closes and returns to normal.
• Theft recovery state is also cleared.
help.s7.photosContent:
When a camera motion alert triggers:

• Photos are **automatically captured** at the moment of detection.
• Captured photos are **transmitted to smartphone in real-time**.
• If network is down, photos are **queued offline** and auto-sent on reconnection.
• Photos include metadata: **detection type**, **change rate**, **GPS coordinates**.
help.s8.title: Location
help.s8.howToCheckContent:
Access location features from the status icons on the main screen.

• A **location request** is sent to the smartphone.
• After a moment, the **current location appears on a map**.
• Location coordinates (latitude/longitude) and **address** are also shown.
• Location update timestamp is displayed.

⚠️ Smartphone must be online with location services enabled.
If smartphone doesn't respond, the **last saved location** is shown.
help.s8.accuracyContent:
Accuracy varies by source:

• 📡 **GPS-based** — Most accurate (within meters)
• 📶 **Wi-Fi-based** — Estimated (hundreds of meters to kilometers)
  ⚠️ Shows "Wi-Fi estimated location" warning.
• 🌐 **IP-based** — Approximate (several kilometers+)
  ⚠️ Shows "IP estimated location" warning.

GPS accuracy may decrease indoors.
Make sure location permission is set to **"Precise location"**.
help.s9.title: Network Info
help.s9.infoContent:
View detailed **network status** of the laptop:

• 🟢 **Connection Status** — Online / Offline
• 🌐 **IP Address** — Currently assigned IP
• 📡 **Connection Type** — Wi-Fi, Ethernet, etc.
• ⚡ **Speed** — Downlink speed (Mbps)
• ⏱️ **Latency (RTT)** — Network response time (ms)
• 📊 **Effective Connection Grade** — slow-2g, 2g, 3g, 4g, etc.

Based on the browser's **Network Information API**.
Some browsers may show limited information.
help.s10.title: Camouflage Mode
help.s10.whatContent:
When camouflage mode is activated, the laptop screen turns **completely black**.

• Appears as if the monitor is turned off.
• MeerCOP is invisible even while monitoring is active.
• All keyboard inputs are blocked — the app cannot be operated.
• Mouse cursor is hidden.
• On alert, notifications are sent **in the background** without screen changes.
• A thief opening the laptop **cannot tell** that monitoring is running.
help.s10.howToUseContent:
**Activate:**
Turn **Camouflage Mode toggle ON** in the smartphone app.
The laptop screen turns black immediately.

**Deactivate:**
Turn camouflage mode **OFF** in the smartphone app.
It cannot be deactivated directly on the laptop.

💡 **Tip**: Combine with Theft Recovery Mode for maximum effectiveness.
While the thief uses the laptop, location and photos are automatically collected.
help.s11.title: Theft Recovery Mode
help.s11.whatContent:
**Location tracking and evidence collection** continue even after laptop theft.

• If the browser closes or restarts during an alert, **alarm state auto-restores**.
• GPS location is **periodically updated**.
• Camera **periodically captures photos** and transmits to smartphone.
• Alarm sound continues playing.
help.s11.howItWorksContent:
**Automatic behavior:**
1. Alert state is automatically recorded when triggered.
2. If browser closes, alarm **auto-restores** when reopened.
3. Location info is periodically updated.
4. Camera photos are continuously captured and transmitted.

**Deactivation:**
• Dismissing the alert from smartphone also clears theft recovery.
• Entering PIN on laptop also clears it.

💡 **Tip**: Combine with camouflage mode — the thief won't know evidence is being collected.
Collected info can be used as **evidence for police reports**.
help.s12.title: Multi-Device Management
help.s12.addDeviceContent:
Register **multiple computers** with a single serial number.

1. Open MeerCOP web app on the new computer.
2. Enter the **same serial number**.
3. Set a **different device name** (e.g., "Office PC", "Home Laptop").
4. Tap Connect to register the new device.
help.s12.switchDeviceContent:
View the **status of all registered devices** in the smartphone app.

• Select a device from the dropdown at the top.
• The selected device's status, camera, location, and settings are displayed.
• Monitor each device individually.
help.s12.changeNameContent:
Tap the **device name badge** on the main screen to change the name.

• Enter a new name and press **Confirm**.
• Duplicate names with other devices are not allowed.
• The changed name reflects immediately on the smartphone app.
• The name persists even after logout.
help.s13.title: Side Menu
help.s13.profileContent:
Opening the side menu shows:

• 👤 **Device Name** — Name of the currently connected device
• 🔑 **Serial Number** — The serial number used to log in
• 👑 **Membership** — Current plan information
help.s13.membershipContent:
Membership tiers based on your serial number:

• **1-Day Trial** — Free trial (limited features)
• **Basic Plan** — Basic features available
• **Premium Plan** — All features available

Upgrade your membership on the MeerCOP website.
help.s13.helpMenuContent:
This is the screen you're viewing now.

• Contains **detailed descriptions** of all app features.
• Find common troubleshooting in **FAQ** section.
• If issues persist, contact **meercop.official@gmail.com**.
help.s13.logoutContent:
Pressing **Logout**:

• Ends the current session.
• Deletes saved serial number and authentication data.
• Device status changes to **offline**.
• Monitoring stops.
• Settings like device name are retained on server — restored on next login.

⚠️ After logout, the device continues to show as **offline** in the smartphone app.
help.s14.title: Sound Settings
help.s14.headerSoundContent:
Tap the **speaker icon** on the top-right header to toggle alarm sound.

• 🔊 **ON** — Selected alarm sound plays when alert triggers.
• 🔇 **OFF** — No sound on alert; only smartphone notification is sent.

This is separate from volume.
Volume is adjusted via the slider in the settings panel.
help.s15.title: Motion Test
help.s15.whatContent:
A feature to **preview camera motion detection sensitivity**.

Tap the **"🔬 Motion Test"** link under Camera Motion in the settings panel to open the dedicated test screen.
help.s15.howToUseContent:
The Motion Test screen shows:

• 📹 **Camera Feed** — Real-time webcam video
• 🔴 **Change Detection Visualization** — Detected change areas highlighted in red
• 📊 **Real-time Status** — Current change rate, consecutive detections, max change rate
• ⚙️ **Sensitivity Settings** — Sensitive/Normal/Insensitive, consecutive frames, cooldown time
• 📋 **Event Log** — Detection event history

**How to use:**
1. Press **▶ Start Test** to activate the camera.
2. Move in front of the camera to check detection response.
3. Adjust sensitivity to find your preferred level.
4. Press **⏹ Stop** to end the test.
help.faq.a1:
You can find your serial number in the **Settings** menu of the MeerCOP smartphone app.
If you can't access the app, contact **meercop.official@gmail.com** for serial number recovery.
help.faq.a2:
Check the following in order:

1. Verify the webcam is **physically connected**
2. Check if **other apps** (Zoom, Teams, etc.) are using the camera → close them
3. Click the **lock icon next to the address bar** → **Allow** camera permission
4. **Completely close** the browser and relaunch
5. Check if camera drivers are properly installed in **Device Manager**
help.faq.a3:
Check the following:

1. Verify the **speaker icon** in the header is ON (🔊)
2. Check that the **volume slider** in settings is not 0%
3. Ensure **system volume** on the computer is turned on
4. Check if the browser has **sound playback blocked** (look for 🔇 on the tab icon)
5. Select an alarm sound in settings and press **▶** to test preview
help.faq.q4: Can't see camera video from smartphone
help.faq.a4:
Check the following:

1. Verify the laptop **camera status icon** is green
2. Confirm the laptop is **online**
3. Ensure both laptop and smartphone are **connected to the internet**
4. **Close and reopen** the camera view
5. **Refresh** the laptop browser

💡 WebRTC connections may take time depending on network conditions.
help.faq.a5:
Location accuracy varies by source:

• GPS accuracy may decrease **indoors**.
• **Wi-Fi-based** location can be off by hundreds of meters to kilometers.
• **IP-based** location can differ by several kilometers or more.

To improve:
1. Set smartphone **location service** to "**Precise location**"
2. Check location where **GPS signal** is strong
3. Press the **Update** button to request the latest location
help.faq.a6:
Try the following:

1. Change camera sensitivity to **"Insensitive"**
2. **Disable** unnecessary sensors (from smartphone)
3. **Close** other heavy programs or tabs
4. Check if browser **hardware acceleration** is enabled

💡 Camera motion detection uses the most resources. Disable it if camera isn't needed.
help.faq.a7:
Check the following:

1. Verify MeerCOP web app is **running** on the laptop
2. Confirm the laptop is **connected to the internet**
3. Check that the browser tab is **active** (some browsers throttle inactive tabs)

If the app is running, it will automatically go **online** shortly.
If it persists, **refresh** the browser.
help.faq.q8: What is the "Use a different serial" button?
help.faq.a8:
Used when you want to log in with a different serial number while auto-logged in via **Remember Me**.

• Pressing this button deletes saved serial data and lets you enter new credentials.
• The existing device's data remains on the server.
help.faq.a9:
Smartphone and laptop communicate via **real-time channels**.

• Smartphone may show as temporarily offline when the app goes to **background**.
• Smartphone **battery saver mode** may restrict the app.
• Keep the smartphone app in **foreground** for stable connection.
• Unstable internet may cause temporary disconnections.
help.faq.q10: Can't operate laptop in camouflage mode
help.faq.a10:
This is **normal behavior**.

Camouflage mode blacks out the screen and **blocks all keyboard/mouse inputs**.
Camouflage mode cannot be deactivated from the laptop.

**To deactivate:**
Turn **Camouflage Mode OFF** in the MeerCOP smartphone app.
The laptop screen returns to normal immediately.
help.faq.q11: What are the benefits of installing as PWA?
help.faq.a11:
Installing MeerCOP as a **PWA (Progressive Web App)**:

• **App icon** added to desktop for quick access
• Runs **without address bar** in full screen
• Runs in an **independent window** like a native app
• Basic features available offline

**How to install:**
Chrome: Click **⊕ icon** on the right of address bar → "Install"
Edge: Click **⊕ icon** on the right of address bar → "Install app"
help.faq.q12: I see a serial expiration message
help.faq.a12:
Your serial number has expired.

• **1-Day Trial** plan expires 24 hours after registration.
• You will be automatically logged out on expiration.
• **Renew or upgrade** your plan on the MeerCOP website.
• Contact: **meercop.official@gmail.com**
help.faq.q13: How do I hide or show the mascot (character)?
help.faq.a13:
Go to Settings (⚙️) → **Character Display** toggle.

• **ON**: MeerCOP character and speech bubble are shown on the main screen.
• **OFF**: Character and speech bubble are hidden.

The bottom status bar remains visible even when the character is hidden, so you can always check your security status.
Settings are auto-saved.
settings.mascotDisplayDesc: Show/hide mascot character and speech bubble
camera.title: Camera
camera.error.timeout:
Camera connection timed out.

If no permission popup appeared, click the camera icon in the address bar to allow access.
camera.error.notAllowed:
Camera permission denied.

Click the lock icon next to the address bar to allow camera access.
camera.error.notFound:
Camera not found.

• Check if the camera is connected
• Check if another app is using the camera
• Try restarting the browser
camera.error.notReadable:
Cannot access camera.

• Close the camera in other apps or tabs
• Check the camera connection
camera.error.overconstrained:
Cannot apply camera settings.

Try using a different camera.
camera.error.abort:
Camera connection was interrupted.

Please try again.
camera.error.security:
Cannot use camera due to security settings.

HTTPS connection is required.
camera.error.disconnected:
Camera disconnected.

Please reconnect the camera and try again.
camera.error.notSupported: This browser does not support camera.
camera.error.default:
Cannot start camera.

Please try again.
notification.title: Notifications
network.title: Network Info
network.footer: 📡 Based on Browser Network Information API
location.title: Location
location.lastKnown: Smartphone not responding. Showing last saved location.
location.noResponse:
Smartphone not responding to location request.
Please check if the app is running.
location.wifiWarning: 📶 Wi-Fi estimated location — may differ by hundreds of meters to kilometers
location.ipWarning: 🌐 IP estimated location — may differ by several kilometers
motion.title: 🔬 Motion Detection Test
motion.emptyLog: Events will appear when test starts
deviceSettings.title: Device Settings
deviceSettings.sensorHint: 💡 Detection sensor settings (camera, keyboard, mouse, USB, etc.) are managed from the smartphone app.
alarmSelector.title: Select Alarm Sound
permission.title: Permissions Required
permission.subtitle: These permissions are needed for the app to work properly
permission.camera.name: Camera
permission.camera.desc: Required for motion detection and photo capture
permission.microphone.name: Microphone
